Composition.Programmer 1:
Implement the backend code in app.py
- Develop the main backend Python application using Flask.
- The website should be accessible via local port 5000, with the first page accessible at route '/' (Dashboard page).
- Implement routing for all eight pages  Dashboard, Property Search, Property Details, Property Inquiry, My Inquiries, My Favorites, Agent Directory, and Locations.
- Implement file read/write operations for all data files (properties.txt, locations.txt, inquiries.txt, favorites.txt, agents.txt) starting from the first line, using plain text without encryption.
- Implement business logic to support all required functionalities  browsing, searching, filtering, viewing details, submitting inquiries, managing favorites, and agent/location listings.
- Ensure all routes correspond to frontend templates in the templates/ directory.
Composition.Programmer 2:
Develop all frontend HTML templates in the templates/ directory
- Create the following HTML files using a Python web framework templating engine (e.g., Jinja2) 
  - dashboard.html
  - property_search.html
  - property_details.html
  - property_inquiry.html
  - my_inquiries.html
  - my_favorites.html
  - agent_directory.html
  - locations.html
- Each template must implement the page design and elements as specified in the requirements document.
- All URLs in the frontend code should be implemented as Flask routes, not direct HTML file names.
- Ensure the frontend integrates with backend data and supports user interactions as per the requirements.
Composition.Programmer 3:
Develop static assets in the static/ directory
- Create CSS styles in static/css/styles.css for consistent styling across all pages.
- Develop JavaScript in static/js/scripts.js to handle client-side interactivity such as button actions, filtering, and navigation.
- Organize any property or agent images (if applicable) in static/images/.
- Ensure static assets support and enhance the user experience on all frontend pages.
